{{Quote|I am Tal Fraan, proctor cogent to the viceroy.|Tal Fraan to [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]|<ref name="BFC3">''[[Tyrant's Test]]''</ref>}}
'''Tal Fraan''' was a male [[Yevetha]], unusual in his [[Speciesism|xenophobic]] society for his interest in the attitudes and behavior of other species.

Tal Fraan appears to have pursued a military career, serving in the garrison on the prison moon of [[Pa'aal]] and the crews of the warships [[Valorous (Victory-class)|''Devotion of Yevetha'']] and [[Rakehell (Sorannan-class)|''Glory of Yevetha'']]. His postings on Pa'aal and aboard ''Devotion'' placed him in close proximity with the prisoners who were forced to assist the Yevetha with the reverse-engineering of captured [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] technology, and gave him an extensive opportunity to study [[Human/Legends|Human]] behavior.<ref name="BFC2">''[[Shield of Lies]]''</ref>

Subsequently, while serving aboard ''Glory'', he played a role in planning the defense of the [[Doornik-319 system]], also known to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] as [[Doornik-319|Morning's Bell]], and to the Yevetha themselves as [[Doornik-319|Preza]]. Based on his study of the prisoners' psychology, Tal Fraan appears to have originated the idea of placing hostages aboard Yevethan warships to act as human shields against the [[New Republic Fifth Fleet]], a tactic which led to the Yevethan victory at the [[Battle of Doornik-319]]. In response, Tal Fraan was promoted by ''Glory''{{'s}} commanding [[Primate (Yevethan rank)|primate]] to a higher rank within the crew. This was probably the point at which he attained the position of [[Proctor (Yevethan)|proctor]].<ref name="BFC2" />

The young proctor's role came to the attention of [[Nil Spaar]], the ruling [[Viceroy/Legends|Viceroy]] of the Yevetha, who summoned Tal Fraan from the ''Glory'' to serve as his personal aide and advisor.<ref name="BFC2" /> In this role, he took the title of "proctor cogent".<ref name="BFC3" />

An encounter with [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] made Tal Fraan realize that he had miscalculated the effect that direct displays of aggression against hostages would have on the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Noting the New Republic's apparent lack of appetite for confrontation with the Imperial [[Replacement warlord|warlords]], he now suggested, as an alternative, that the Yevetha feign an alliance with a powerful [[Moff/Legends|Moff]]. Instead of pausing, however, the [[New Republic Fifth Fleet]] advanced into Yevethan space, discovering and destroying the [[Black Nine]] shipyard at the [[Battle of ILC-905]].<ref name="BFC3" />

This failure resulted in Tal Fraan's death at the hands of Nil Spaar who decapitated him with his [[dew claw]]s.<ref name="BFC3" />
